problem: A count (e.g. severity_counts, occurrence_counts) is reported without stating whether it covers WCAG-only findings or all categories, or whether it's per-page or batch-aggregated — a reader can silently misinterpret its scope.
evidence: Startkorpus item from issue #511 ("Zaehler ohne Scope"); addressed by the summary.severity_counts_scope and summary.occurrence_counts_scope string fields (src/output/json.rs:193,196,449,452), always emitted as e.g. "wcag_only" alongside the counts themselves.
expected: Every count field whose scope is not self-evident from its name carries an explicit sibling *_scope field, and CLAUDE.md documents the convention (severity_counts stays WCAG-only; occurrence_counts/violation_count/violated_rule_count cover all categories).
regression: src/output/json/tests.rs asserts unified.summary.occurrence_counts_scope == "wcag_only" is present on generated reports (existing test, not written this session).
counter_examples: A count field whose name is already unambiguous (e.g. sample.audited, a URL count) does not need a *_scope sibling and must not be flagged for lacking one.
